item.title: word_count
item.description: Vault Stats This vault currently contains 14,446 words across all notes.

item.title: Unlinked_Pages
item.description: Unlinked Notes Recently_Updated Buggy choke Baseball choke Two grip choke on turtle Close guard overhook stiff Crusifix Straight ankle lock Grip break on upper back Cross collar grip break to takedown Collar grip break downward Double ankle grab pass Double under Toreando Leg drag S grip Lapel choke...

item.title: Recently_Updated
item.description: Recently Updated Notes K guard sweep Back, standing Gameplan README Empty_Pages Formatting index Banana split Kimura & Americana Three sisters .

item.title: Thumb and cuff grip
item.description: Description Grip where you have only the thumb inside the lapel or similar clothing.

item.title: K guard sweep
item.description: General Control is exactly the same as in K guard, and the sweep direction is towards opponents 10-11 o’clock (assuming control on left leg as usual) After the opponent is swept to their knees you have way too many options to pursue: Calf slicer Heel hook Crab ride .

item.title: K guard
item.description: General Openguard where two conditions are met: Arm has underhook behind opponents knee Knee is on the mat close to behind opponents knee Important distinction is that K guard can be played when opponent is in your Closed guard, Standing or even sitting.

item.title: Back, standing
item.description: Possibly after Arm drag or otherwise you both are standing and you have obtained opponents back.

item.title: Arm drag
item.description: Both arms on opponents single arm and sweeping motion downward, forcing the opponent to lose balance and likely post with knee and/or other hand Control sleeve with “inner” arm in downward motion and transition to Single leg “Hopping” around can be stopped by hooking behind the leg Can be used as pa...

item.title: Feinting
item.description: Doing an action of revealing and opening in order to trick opponent to act a certain way.

item.title: Stacking
item.description: The general concept of applying downward pressure with gravity to seem heavier and allows for effective Passing techniques.
